history: The client has a history of working overseas and developing a website related to his work. He has a recurrent pattern of initiating new projects but tends to abandon them when faced with difficulties. He expressed frustration with his medication situation and a longing for a time when he felt more capable and in control. He has family-related difficulties, including writing a personal letter as part of a forgiveness process with his father.
intermediate_belief: If I encounter difficulties in my projects, then I am not capable of succeeding.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't handle challenges, then I will never be able to achieve my goals.
coping_strategies: The client tends to abandon projects when faced with difficulties. He also expresses frustration and stress over medication and financial concerns, which may lead to avoidance of addressing these issues directly.
situation: Working on a personal project involving the development of a website.
auto_thought: I can't handle this; it's going to fail like before.
behavior: He becomes overwhelmed and considers abandoning the project. He also expresses frustration about his medication and financial situation.
